mapping.a_element: cause-specific hazard as the intensity of an event-time counting process
mapping.b_element: birth/death/mutation event rates governing the individual-based Markov process

mapping.a_element: continuous-time stochastic event process underlying the data
mapping.b_element: continuous-time Markov jump process of the population model

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is censoring-invariant nonparametric estimation of a survival-weighted rate summary (ACSH) under competing/semi-competing risks, centered on removing dependence on the censoring-time distribution; Paper B's machinery is deriving scaling limits (in time, population size, and mutation rate/step) of stochastic individual-based population models for adaptive dynamics. Beyond the generic fact that both involve continuous-time event rates/intensities, there is no role-for-role correspondence: A has no scaling limits or mutations and B has no censoring, competing risks, or estimation of a hazard summary, so the transferable machinery differs.
